_comment: FFO (French Federation of Othello) endgame test suite, problems #40-#49.
Compiled originally by Marc Tastet (FFO). This file was NOT hand-typed from
memory: the board strings and expected exact scores below were fetched from
two independent, cross-checking public sources and verified to match exactly:
1. http://radagast.se/othello/ffotest.zip (Gunnar Andersson's official FFO
test suite mirror) -> end40.pos .. end49.pos, which give the board string
and the side to move (e.g. end40.pos: 'Black').
2. https://github.com/abulmo/edax-reversi/blob/master/problem/fforum-40-59.obf
(Edax engine's bundled OBF problem file), which gives the same 64-char
board strings plus a move/score list per position; the first (highest)
score in each position's list is that position's exact game-theoretic
value for the side to move.
The board strings from source (1) and source (2) were compared character by
character and are identical for all 10 positions; the side-to-move letter
(source 1: 'Black'/'White') also matches source (2)'s trailing 'X'/'O' marker.
Board string encoding: 64 characters, one per square, in row-major order
a1,b1,...,h1,a2,...,h8 (i.e. character index i corresponds to bit index i of
engine::bitboard::Board, per that module's documented index = rank0*8+file
convention). 'X' = black, 'O' = white, '-' = empty. This was verified by
decoding the well-known OBF standard start-position string
checking it reproduces engine::bitboard::Board::initial() exactly (O at d4
index27, X at e4 index28, X at d5 index35, O at e5 index36).
expected_score is the exact final disc-difference (1 disc = 1, matching the
return convention of engine::endgame::solve_exact) from the point of view of
side_to_move, i.e. the maximum score achievable by side_to_move with best
play by both sides for the remainder of the game.
Only problems #40-#49 (the minimum required by tasks/T009-ffo-bench.md) are
included; #50-#59 were not added because a preliminary timing check showed
this project's current solve_exact (a plain alpha-beta + TT solver with no
MPC / stable-disc pruning, see engine/src/endgame.rs) already takes a
non-trivial amount of time on some #40-#49 positions, and #50-#59 have even
more empty squares (27-34) which would make the bundled test suite too slow
to run routinely.
